today_summary: COMEX gold (GCM26) advances to $4,380 with ATM IV at 21.16%, as the aggregate dealer GEX turns positive for the first time in five sessions — flipping from −7.07 (Jun 17) to +41.04. PC OI ratio edged down to 0.61 with call volume surging to 11,976 vs put volume of 7,089, reaffirming the bullish flow skew. Max pain at $4,900 sits $519 above spot (40 DTE), maintaining a structural expiry pull upward. 1σ daily move = $58, bracketing today's session to roughly $4,322–$4,439 absent a macro catalyst.
five_day_thesis: The 5-day arc from Jun 11–18 tells a clean vol-compression-into-rally story: spot climbed $246 (+5.96%) from $4,134 to $4,380 while ATM IV was crushed 4.33pp (25.49% → 21.16%), meaning the options market absorbed the entire move without repricing risk higher — a hallmark of trend conviction rather than speculative blow-off. The most structurally significant shift is the GEX regime change: on Jun 11, aggregate net GEX was −290.64 (dealers deeply short gamma, amplifying every tick); by Jun 18 it crossed to +41.04, the first positive reading in the window, as dealers covered short-gamma exposure at progressively higher strikes (flip strike migrated 3310 → 3295 → 3485 → 3505). This means the daily volatility amplifier that turbocharged the mid-June selloff is now turned off — the global dealer posture has shifted to buy-dips, sell-rips. Max pain drifted only −$85 (4985 → 4900) while spot rallied $246, so the spot-to-max-pain gap actually narrowed from $851 to $519: expiry gravity is pulling price toward $4,900 with growing force over the remaining 40 DTE. The $4,000 put wall has been structurally immovable across all five sessions (OI consistently the largest in the chain), though it shed 1,080 contracts on Jun 18 (4,935 → 3,855) — a one-day erosion worth watching for confirmation it is unwinding rather than rolling. The $5,370 call ceiling (8,010 OI) has not moved a single contract in five sessions; dealers remain short-call there and will hedge any approach aggressively.
what_changed: GEX sign flipped positive (+41.04) for the first time in 5 sessions; aggregate dealer posture crossed from short-gamma (amplifier) to long-gamma (stabilizer).
ATM IV +0.24pp to 21.16% — a tiny uptick after 4.33pp compression this week; IV remains near the 5-session low, confirming trend-mode not fear-mode.
Max pain held at $4,900 (Jun 18 vs Jun 17's $4,875); the minor intraday drift back up reflects fresh call selling above spot.
$4,000 put wall OI fell 1,080 contracts (4,935 → 3,855 on Jun 18) — largest single-session drop this week; monitor for structural floor erosion vs. roll to higher strikes.
Call volume spiked to 11,976 (vs 7,773 Jun 17); put volume jumped to 7,089 (vs 3,378) — elevated two-sided flow signals the market is actively repositioning, not drifting.
Total call OI +643 to 111,426 while put OI declined −469 to 68,445; call accumulation outpacing puts pushes PCR to 0.61, a fresh 5-session low.
prediction: Mild upward bias with dealer-supported consolidation in the $4,355–$4,400 corridor. The local GEX pocket around $4,355–$4,390 is net-positive (peak: +7.80 at $4,355), creating a dealer buy-the-dip dynamic that should cushion any intraday weakness. Above $4,400 the net GEX turns negative (−1.47 at $4,400, −7.02 at $4,450), so a sustained break above $4,400 would shift dealers to short-gamma hedging mode and could accelerate toward $4,450–$4,500. Below $4,325 (net GEX −5.37, the largest negative pocket near spot), price enters dealer-short territory with no major put wall until $4,150 (2,447 OI) and then $4,000 (3,855 OI). With the global GEX now positive and IV compressed, the path of least resistance remains upward toward max pain at $4,900 over the next 40 DTE, with today's session likely grinding $4,340–$4,440.

day_trade_cta.bias_note: Global GEX turned positive for first time this week; dealers have flipped from short-gamma amplifiers to long-gamma stabilizers. Spot sits in a net-positive GEX pocket ($4,355–$4,390), meaning passive dealer buying absorbs dips. Max pain at $4,900 is the structural magnet for June expiry.
day_trade_cta.entry_zone_note: Peak positive net GEX at $4,355 (+7.80) and $4,385 (+1.13) bracket current spot; dip entries here receive the strongest passive dealer support in the near-spot range. The 1σ lower band is $4,322 — avoid longs below that without confirmation.
day_trade_cta.pivot_note: $4,325 carries the heaviest negative net GEX cluster near spot (−5.37, put_gex −5.99); below here dealers switch to hedging shorts, removing the buy-the-dip bid and opening a fast move toward $4,300 (net GEX −2.65) and then $4,270.
day_trade_cta.stop_note: Clears $4,300 round-number support (call_gex 19.87 / put_gex −22.52, net −2.65). A close below $4,295 means the dominant ATM GEX cluster has failed and dealer flows have turned net-negative; risk of acceleration to $4,250 (net GEX −4.75) opens.
day_trade_cta.targets_note: $4,440 = upper 1σ boundary ($4,380 + $58) and first meaningful call GEX cluster ($4,450 net −7.02); a break above shifts dealer hedging from dampening to amplifying, targeting $4,500. $4,500 is the next clean round-number level before the $4,700 call OI wall (4,860 contracts).
